how_it_works: High-severity findings contribute the most to the score.
Injected memory and repeated suspicious behaviour can increase the score significantly.
The final score is capped at 100.

commonly_reviewed_sections.title: Processes
commonly_reviewed_sections.note: Review running processes, suspicious image names, parent-child relationships, and shell activity.

commonly_reviewed_sections.title: Process Tree
commonly_reviewed_sections.note: Review parent-child relationships to identify suspicious process spawning.

commonly_reviewed_sections.title: Command Lines
commonly_reviewed_sections.note: Review executed paths, Temp/AppData/Downloads usage, and suspicious command invocations.

commonly_reviewed_sections.title: Injected Memory
commonly_reviewed_sections.note: Review executable writable memory regions and repeated hits in the same process.

commonly_reviewed_sections.title: Network Activity
commonly_reviewed_sections.note: Review suspicious ports, remote addresses, and associated processes.

commonly_reviewed_sections.title: DLL Review
commonly_reviewed_sections.note: Review loaded DLLs for unusual modules and unexpected injection artifacts.

commonly_reviewed_sections.title: VAD Review
commonly_reviewed_sections.note: Review memory regions and protections for anomalous mappings.

recommendations: Prioritize this memory image for immediate analyst review.
Prioritize investigation of cmd.exe (PID 1984) due to highest cumulative risk contribution.
Investigate injected-memory hits in Malfind and correlate them with the affected processes.
Network visibility was limited because NetScan was unsupported for this dump.
Review the process tree to confirm suspicious parent-child execution chains.
